about_public_record.about_summary_narrative:
Crista Black is an education insider trustee who brings a career in public school instruction and curriculum to the Longview ISD board. An LISD graduate herself, her professional career includes classroom teaching in Frisco ISD (beginning circa 2001) and progressively responsible roles in Pine Tree ISD, where she served as teacher, instructional specialist, and ELA curriculum coordinator.

Outside the classroom, Black has been deeply embedded in Longview-area civic and volunteer organizations. She served as President of the Junior League of Longview and has been active in the East Texas Literacy Council and Heartisans Marketplace. Within the LISD orbit, she held leadership roles in the Hudson PEP Elementary PTO and Foster Middle School PTO before being elected to the board.

Black was elected unopposed to Place 4 in May 2023, succeeding Ginia Northcutt, and currently serves as Assistant Secretary of the board. She also serves as President of the LISD Education Foundation, a dual role that presents a disclosure question: the Foundation raises and distributes funds that benefit the district she governs, and voters should understand how that relationship is managed.

Her ELA curriculum background makes her positions on book policies, content standards, and instructional materials especially important to track. The cross-district employment question (Pine Tree ISD employment concurrent with LISD governance) is another area warranting transparency.

No negative public-record items have surfaced as of this research date. The appropriate next step is the mechanical records pull described in the workflow, not speculation.

notable_statements.quote_or_paraphrase: Said she recognized the significance of public education and would work collaboratively as a trustee.

education_policy_positions.bond_and_budget: Part of board majority that reduced M&O+I&S tax rate from $1.3276 (2022) to $1.1422 (2023-24)

red_flags.description: Serves as President of LISD Education Foundation while sitting on LISD Board of Trustees. Dual role creates potential for self-dealing or preferential treatment.
Employed by Pine Tree ISD while governing Longview ISD. Cross-district employment creates potential conflicts in competitive enrollment and policy contexts.

summary: Crista Black is the incumbent Place 4 trustee on the Longview ISD Board. LISD graduate, career educator with stops in Frisco ISD and Pine Tree ISD (teacher, instructional specialist, ELA curriculum coordinator). Active civic volunteer: Junior League of Longview (Former President), East Texas Literacy Council, Heartisans Marketplace, Hudson PEP Elementary PTO, Foster Middle School PTO. Elected unopposed in 2023 succeeding Ginia Northcutt. Serves as Assistant Secretary of the board and President of the LISD Education Foundation.
analyst_notes: Education-career trustee with deep curriculum expertise (ELA focus). Dual role as Education Foundation President and LISD Trustee warrants disclosure scrutiny. Cross-district employment at Pine Tree ISD while governing LISD is a secondary transparency question. No strongly partisan public footprint located in open sources as of research date. Priority next moves: confirm 2026 filing, locate social media accounts, confirm current employer, pull campaign finance, confirm primary voting history, identify policy positions on books/vouchers/gender, pull Education Foundation 990s.
